documentation_impact.rationale: Phase 3 step 6 implements the worktree and env-isolation packages: GitWorktreePool with persisted pool state and Q7 single-slot guard (@pancreator/worktree), and PortRegistryEnvIsolation backed by an atomic JSON registry under .pan/sandboxes/ (@pancreator/env-isolation). Updates the two feature task lists.

deferred_items.rationale: Full EnvIsolation (DB names, compose project, .env.pan, hooks) is intentionally deferred; this slice ships port reservation only. Tracked in the feature task list.
Real git worktree integration tests are deferred to a CI slice with sandboxed clones; unit tests use the in-memory GitOps double.
Cross-process locking for the pool state and port registry is deferred; current writes are atomic but rely on single-runtime use. TOCTOU mitigation is a later hardening slice.
LLM-judge README contracts use TBD-on-commit content hashes and require the runner panel; deferred to the contract runner pass.

policy_alignment.agents_md_alignment: Slice stages diffs locally, preserves the human gate at the phase boundary, and respects the no-horizontal-primitive-deps rule.
policy_alignment.constitution_alignment: Slice keeps worktree and isolation artifacts traceable while limiting scope to the declared package boundary.
policy_alignment.prd_alignment: Slice satisfies PRD §11 worktree and env-isolation responsibilities; closes US-6 silent-collision gap by failing duplicate port allocation across tasks; respects Q7 single-pipeline-at-a-time guard.
